ENVELOPE_WALL: External wall assembly defining thermal resistance and thermal mass of the building envelope.
ENVELOPE_ROOF: Roof assembly including insulation layers and waterproofing; dominant heat loss path in top-floor zones.
ENVELOPE_WINDOW: Glazing system combining frame, glass layers, and g-value; governs solar gains and conduction losses.
ENVELOPE_FLOOR: Ground-floor slab assembly setting base insulation and thermal bridge at the building perimeter.
ENVELOPE_SHADING: Solar shading element (overhang, blind, or coating) controlling direct solar gains through glazed surfaces.
ENVELOPE_MASS: Internal thermal mass assembly determining heat storage capacity and temperature lag within conditioned spaces.
ENVELOPE_TIGHTNESS: Air-tightness layer specifying infiltration rate; affects both heat losses and indoor air quality.
HVAC_HEATING: Space-heating terminal system (radiators, underfloor, air coils) distributing heat to conditioned zones.
HVAC_COOLING: Space-cooling terminal system (fan coils, radiant panels, air diffusers) distributing cooling to zones.
HVAC_HOTWATER: Domestic hot water distribution circuit within the building, from storage or instantaneous source to tap.
HVAC_CONTROLLER: Building control system governing heating/cooling setpoints, schedules, and system switching logic.
HVAC_VENTILATION: Mechanical ventilation unit providing fresh-air supply, with optional heat recovery (HRV/ERV).
SUPPLY_HEATING: Primary heat generation plant (boiler, heat pump, district heat) serving the building heating circuit.
SUPPLY_HOTWATER: Primary heat source dedicated to domestic hot water production, separate from space heating where required.
SUPPLY_COOLING: Primary cooling generation (chiller, reversible heat pump, district cooling) serving the cooling circuit.
SUPPLY_ELECTRICITY: Primary electricity source: grid connection and/or on-site generation (PV, CHP) serving building loads.
GRID: Electricity supplied from the national grid; emissions and cost factors follow DE grid mix parameters.
NATURALGAS: Natural gas from the distribution network; used by boilers, CHP, and gas-fired heat pumps.
OIL: Fuel oil (heating oil) delivered to on-site storage; used by oil-fired boilers and burners.
SOLAR: Solar irradiation as primary energy input for solar thermal collectors and photovoltaic panels.
DRYBIOMASS: Solid dry biomass (wood pellets, chips); used by biomass boilers as a low-carbon heat source.
WOOD: Firewood or wood logs; used by stoves and log boilers, typically for smaller residential loads.
